mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, marginal increases in industrial production reflect capacity utilization peaks and pricing power, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative returns in more than 65% of late-cycle observations, or the effect reverses when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or GDP growth periods show no significant return premium compared to contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI growth crosses above 3.0%, rising input costs and anticipated monetary tightening compress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of joint occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declining housing starts signal credit market stress and reduced construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of tightening cycle observations, or housing starts lose predictive significance when mortgage rates exceed 6%.
